title: Is winter fuel U-turn a leadership 'fiasco' for Labour?
summary: Did someone say U-turn? Ed Balls and George Osborne have been saying it for a while, actually, when it comes to the matter of the controversial winter fuel tax cut. Now that the government has walked it back, will it do the trick and soothe voters and ease tensions with Labour MPs? Ed thinks the approach is вҖңpolitically and technically messyвҖқ. George, for his part, thinks itвҖҷs nothing short of a damaging вҖңfiascoвҖқ for Labour.В  Meanwhile, David Lammy announced this week the suspension of trade talks with Israel, in light of the escalating violence in Gaza. Does this signal a shift in the UK-Israel relations? And will it make much difference to the crisis if it does? George isnвҖҷt so sureвҖҰВ  Plus, the pair discuss the newly-minted, post-Brexit deal with the EU - did Britain make the right calls and the right concessions? And a trip down banking regulation memory lane: is it time to wind back bank ringfencing, an ongoing legacy from the Osborne era? And a reminder, weвҖҷre doing our first Political Currency live show - weвҖҷll be taking to the stage on Thursday 5th June 2025 at SXSW London. Want a chance to grab a FREE ticket and a pass to the WHOLE six day business conference, worth ВЈ600?! Sign up to be a Kitchen Cabinet member to hear how you can get your hands on a ticket!В  To get episodes early and ad-free, join Political Currency Gold or the Kitchen Cabinet: В рҹ‘ү patreon.com/politicalcurrency  рҹ‘ү Apple Podcasts Please note: Kitchen Cabinet is only available via Patreon. title: "Chancellor Gordon Brown" Returns
summary: Sky NewsвҖҷ Sam Coates and PoliticoвҖҷs Anne McElvoy serve up their essential guide to the day in British politics.В В  В  As Sir Keir Starmer waves goodbye to the Chagos Islands and his planned cuts to the winter fuel allowance, Sam and Anne assess what it all means. В  В  How much вҖңbackseat drivingвҖқ on the winter fuel U-turn has come from former chancellor Gordon Brown? And whatвҖҷs the best way to implement a volte face?В  В  And what do the Gauke reforms, set to be accepted by Justice Secretary Shabana Mahmood, mean for our stretched prison and probation services?

title: Labour takes action over Gaza: what took them so long? вҖ“ Politics Weekly UK
summary: The government has suspended trade talks with Israel and denounced its actions in Gaza. But why are we only now seeing this kind of action and moral outrage? John Harris speaks to Melanie Ward, a Labour MP and former chief executive at Medical Aid for Palestinians. Plus, after Keir StarmerвҖҷs reset with the EU, how does the public feel about the politics of Brexit? Guardian columnist Rafael Behr and Sophie Stowers from More in Common join John to discuss. Help support our independent journalism at theguardian.com/politicspod

title: Gaza and Benefits: is Labour changing course?
summary: Reunited, Coco and Nish are finally back together in the studio to talk U turns, disability benefits, Brexit andвҖҰ gimp masks? ThereвҖҷs a lot of politics to catch up on including some big shifts from the Labour government.В  First on Gaza - Keir Starmer and David Lammy changed tack with fierce criticism of the Israeli government. But critics argue that without an actual arms embargo talking tough with Israel is inadequate. So is it all too little, too late?В  And in another change of direction, the Prime Minister has been listening to voters and his own disgruntled MPs on benefits. He signalled a rethink on one of Labour's most unpopular moves - cutting winter fuel payments for pensioners. But reforms of disability benefits are still on the cards so Nish heads to Westminster where disability activists gathered to lobby their MPs. With a major rebellion on the cards from Labour MPs, can Starmer be steered into a U-turn on this too? Employment minister Alison McGovern explains how sheвҖҷs going to get more young people into work and she has a message for Pod Save the UKвҖҷs disillusioned Labour voters.В  And did you think Brexit bollocks was in the rearview mirror? Well sorry - this week the Brexit undead returned with nonsense we thought was safely consigned to the dustbin of history.В  Guests: Alison McGovern MP Ellie Chowns MP Nadia Whittome MP Meg Thomas, Disability Rights CampaignerВ  Audio Credits: Parliament TV BBC GB News Useful Links:В  https://www.disabilityrightsuk.org/news/mass-lobby-against-benefit-cuts-21st-may-be-there Pod Save the UK is a Reduced Listening production for Crooked Media. title: Starmer's first big U-turn
summary: At PMQs, Kemi Badenoch asked Keir Starmer when вҖңthe inevitable U-turn on winter fuel" would come. Unfortunately for her, heвҖҷd already announced it. Admittedly, it was quietly done. With a bit of hedging and fudging. But if you were following closely, youвҖҷd have heard him commit to a rethink at "the next fiscal statement" to a Labour MP who stood up before the Tory leader. When will the backtrack come into force? And does this alleviate pressure on the Treasury on benefits - or just ramp it up? We speak to the Labour backbencher Brian Leishman, who wants to see his leader go further and faster.  And ahead of a major shakeup in our prisons and justice system - does the government want to reduce prisoner numbers out or principle, or is it just a result of a system on its knees? title: Winter fuel U-turn and a rift at the heart of government
summary: After sustained speculation and a local elections drubbing, Keir Starmer announced today at PMQs that the government will be softening their policy on winter fuel. Whilst it wonвҖҷt come into effect for some time, they have agreed to ensure that вҖҳmore pensioners are eligible for winter fuel payment.вҖҷВ  This comes hours after a memo was mysteriously leaked to the Telegraph. It contains an extensive list of recommendations from Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner to the Treasury, including a set of eight tax rises such as reinstating the pensions lifetime allowance and altering dividend taxes. This amounts to a direct challenge to Rachel ReevesвҖҷs fiscal approach and preference for spending cuts. The bigger story, of course, is what this says about feelings within Labour вҖ“ itвҖҷs clearly not a happy family. Sources suggest that Angela Rayner is uneasy with the direction of travel in the Treasury and speaks for a silent majority in the Labour party who are fed up with defending controversial measures such as winter fuel. So who leaked this memo? Could Rayner be leading a mutiny? And will the U-turn on winter fuel placate the rebels?В  Oscar Edmondson speaks to James Heale and Isabel Hardman. Produced by Oscar Edmondson.

summary: бҹЎ) бһҖбҹ’бһҡбһҹбһҪбһ„бһҖбһ¶бһҡбһ„бһ¶бһҡбһўбҹҶбһ–бһ¶бһңбһ“бһ¶бһңбһұбҹ’бһҷ бһҖбһҳбҹ’бһҳбһҖбһҡбһҡбҹ„бһ„бһ…бһҖбҹ’бһҡ бһҒбһӣбҹҗбһҡбһҹбҹ’бһҸбһ¶бһҡ (Color Star) бһҡбһ„бҹӢбһ…бһ¶бҹҶбһҖбһ¶бһҡбһҠбҹ„бҹҮбһҹбҹ’бһҡбһ¶бһҷбһҡбһ”бһҹбҹӢбһҖбҹ’бһҡбһҹбһҪбһ„бһҮбһ¶бһҳбһҪбһҷбһҖбҹ’бһҡбһ»бһҳбһ бҹҠбһ»бһ“бҹ” бҹў) бһ”бһҖбҹ’бһҹбһҖбҹ’бһҡбҹ…бһҡбһҠбҹ’бһӢбһ¶бһ—бһ·бһ”бһ¶бһӣбһҮбҹҶбһҡбһ»бһүбһұбҹ’бһҷ бһӣбҹ„бһҖ бһ бҹҠбһ»бһ“ бһҳбҹүбһ¶бһҺбҹӮбһҸ бһ”бһүбҹ’бһҮбһјбһ“вҖӢбһўбһ“бһ»бһҹбһүбҹ’бһүбһ¶вҖӢбһўбһ„бҹ’бһӮбһҖбһ¶бһҡбһҹбһ бһ”бҹ’бһҡбһҮбһ¶бһҮбһ¶бһҸбһ·бһҹбҹ’бһҸбһёбһ–бһёбһ…бҹ’бһ”бһ¶бһ”бҹӢбһҹбһҳбһ»бһ‘бҹ’бһҡбһ‘бҹ…бһҡбһҠбҹ’бһӢбһҹбһ—бһ¶бһ•бҹ’бһҸбһӣбҹӢвҖӢбһҹбһ…бҹ’бһ…бһ¶бһ”бҹҗбһ“бһұбҹ’бһҷбһ”бһ¶бһ“бһҶбһ¶бһ”бҹӢбҹ” бҹЈ) вҖӢбһӣбҹ„бһҖ бһ бҹҠбһ»бһ“ бһҹбҹӮбһ“бҹ– бһҠбһҫбһҳбҹ’бһ”бһёбһҖбһ»бҹҶбһұбҹ’бһҷбһҡбһ”бһ”бһ”бҹ’бһҡбһӣбҹҗбһҷ бһ–бһјбһҮбһҹбһ¶бһҹбһ“бҹҚбһңбһ·бһӣвҖӢбһҸбҹ’бһҡбһЎбһ”бҹӢбһҳбһҖбһңбһ·бһүбһ–бһӣбһҡбһҠбҹ’бһӢбһҒбҹ’бһҳбҹӮбһҡбһӮбҹ’бһҡбһ”бҹӢбһҡбһјбһ”бһҸбҹ’бһҡбһјбһңбһ…бһјбһӣбһҡбһҪбһҳбһҗбҹӮбһҡбһҖбҹ’бһҹбһ¶бһҖбһ¶бһҡбһ–бһ¶бһҡбһҹбһ“бҹ’бһҸбһ·бһ—бһ¶бһ–бҹ” бҹӨ) бһҳбһҮбҹ’бһҲбһҳбһҺбҹ’бһҢбһӣвҖӢбһҹбһҳбҹ’бһҡбһ¶бһ”бҹӢбһҖбһ¶бһҡвҖӢбһҹбһ·бһҖбҹ’бһҹбһ¶вҖӢбһўбһ“бҹ’бһҸбһҡбһҮбһ¶бһҸбһ·вҖӢбһ“бһ·бһ„бһҷбһ»бһ‘бҹ’бһ’бһҹбһ¶бһҹбҹ’бһҸбҹ’бһҡвҖӢбһҡбһ”бһҹбҹӢвҖӢбһўбһ¶бһҳбҹҒбһҡбһ·бһҖбһҡбһҖбһғбһҫбһүбһҗбһ¶бҹ– бһ…бһ·бһ“бһҖбҹҶбһ–бһ»бһ„бһҖбһ¶бһ“бҹӢбһҖбһ¶бһ”бҹӢбһ•бҹ’бһҸбһ¶бһ…бҹӢвҖӢбһҳбһ»бһҒвҖӢбһҖбһ“бҹ’бһӣбҹӮбһ„бһҒбҹ’бһӣбҹҮбһ“бҹғбһҳбһјбһӣбһҠбҹ’бһӢбһ¶бһ“вҖӢбһҖбһ„бһ‘бҹҗбһ–вҖӢбһҮбһҫбһ„вҖӢбһ‘бһ№бһҖвҖӢбһҹбһҳбһ»бһ‘бҹ’бһҡбһҡбһ¶бһҳбһҡбһ”бһҹбҹӢбһҒбҹ’бһҳбҹӮбһҡбҹ” вҖӢ бҹҘ) бһ–бһјбһ‘бһёбһ“бһ“бҹ…бһҸбҹӮбһ”бһ“бҹ’бһҸбһ…бҹ’бһҡбһ¶бһ“бһ…бҹ„бһӣбһ”бһ‘бһҲбһ”бҹӢбһ”бһ¶бһүбҹӢбһ”бһҫбһўбҹҠбһ»бһҷбһҖбҹ’бһҡбҹӮбһ“бһҳбһ·бһ“вҖӢбһ–бҹ’бһҡбһҳбһҠбһҖвҖӢбһ‘бҹҗбһ–бһ…бҹҒбһүбһ–бһёвҖӢбһҸбҹҶбһ”бһ“бҹӢбһҠбҹӮбһӣбһҡбһ»бһҹбҹ’бһҹбҹҠбһёбһҖбһ¶бһ“бҹӢбһҖбһ¶бһ”бҹӢбҹ”

В  Economy Leftist British PM Keir Starmer BETRAYS Brexit, Causes Alarm by Signing Agreement With European Union Making HUGE Concessions  	В  Prime Minister, Keir Starmer, has just betrayed Brexit вҖ“ and may have dealt a death blow to the islandвҖҷs fishing industry.  	ThereвҖҷs widespread alarm over the terms of the вҖҳresetвҖҷ deal with the European Union and the huge concessions that he made.  	At a press conference alongside European Commission chief Ursula von der Leyen, Keir Starmer says he is вҖҳmoving onвҖҷ from вҖҳBrexit battlesвҖҷ, and that this deal will put Britain вҖҳback on the world stageвҖҷ. The move follows a sweeping stimulus package announced earlier this month.  	The PeopleвҖҷs Bank of China (PBOC) has lowered its benchmark lending rates for the first time in seven months as part of ongoing efforts to mitigate the impact of US tariffs on its economy.  	The central bank cut the 1-year and 5-year loan prime rates (LPR) by 10 basis points to 3.0% and 3.5%, Source:В  euronews.comВ  US Federal Reserve officials signal rates likely to stay on hold until at least September  	The central bankвҖҷs next three meetings are in June, July and September https://twitter.com/MarketsDotNews/status/1924709190376820961 https://twitter.com/TrumpWarRoom/status/1924818332550639622 В  https://twitter.com/KobeissiLetter/status/1924816670285054349 Trump tax bill will expire on December 31, 2025. and taxes will go up No,

title: UK suspends trade talks with Israel
summary: The British Foreign Secretary, David Lammy, has announced the suspension of negotiations with Israel on a new trade agreement - due to what he called its "intolerable" and "abominable" recent actions in Gaza. The World Health Organisation says two million people in Gaza are starving. As Israel allows some aid in after an eleven week blockade, the British government says it's nowhere near enough. Also on the programme: Tanzania detains two prominent human rights activists who had travelled to Dar es Salaam to observe an opposition leaderвҖҷs treason case. And we'll have an appreciation of a ballet maestro with an iron fist. (Photo: Britain's Foreign Minister David Lammy delivers a statement on the Israel and Hamas ceasefire deal, at the House of Commons, in London, Britain, January 16, 2025. Credit: House of Commons/Handout via REUTERS)

title: TOMMY ROBINSON FREED AS LUCY CONNOLLY REMAINS LOCKED UP FOR TWEET & HER HUSBAND SPEAKS OUT
summary: SHEATH UNDERWEAR - Get 20% off with the code OUTSPOKEN at checkout https://sheath.com The UK state keeps one of our political prisoners locked up into a 284 day stint in jail for a tweet while reluctantly agreeing to release another. But these barbaric sentences prove that there is no free speech in Britain, we are an authoritarian state and Slippery Starmer lied to JD VanceвҖҷs face in the Oval office. WeвҖҷre going to have the coverage you simply will not get from the corrupt and crooked MSM on the cases of Tommy Robinson and Lucy Connolly today. We'll hear exclusively from Lucy's husband Raymond Connolly, the Conservative Councillor, and then our very special Superstar Panel: Ezra Levant, boss of Rebel News who flew from Canada to be at the High Court today, and the Editor of Conservative Woman, who has closely covered both cases, Kathy Gyngell. PLUS: David StarkeyвҖҷs chilling message about the damage mass immigration the MSM simply will not allow you to hear. AND: A bloodbath at ITV. I worked at ITV Daytime for ten years and know the REAL story behind the decision today that effectively sees Lorraine and Loose Women axed - and Good Morning Britain subsumed into ITV News THEN IN THE UNCANCELLED AFTERSHOW: Prince William and Catherine, the Princess of Wales, have revealed they will never speak to Meghan Markle again. WeвҖҷll bring you the latest from our Royal Mastermind Angela Levin. Sign up to watch at www.outspoken.live. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

title: Israel Accuses UK Of Antisemitism Over Sanctions Threat
summary: The UK, France and Canada are threatening Israel with sanctions for blocking aid to Gaza. Plus: A new extinction-level poll for the Tories; and Andrew Tate faces extradition to the UK. With Aaron Bastani and Kieran Andrieu.

title: The Richie Allen Show Tuesday May 20th 2025
summary: Richie Allen with analysis of the day's top news stories. On today's show: Tommy Robinson to be released from prison this week, but no such luck for Lucy Connolly the mum who was jailed over a tweet. The UK government suspends trade talks with Israel over Gaza war conduct. UK supermarket launches wedding gift range for struggling couples. Hunger Games Society? Plus much more.

title: The UK hardens its stance on Israel
summary: The UK has suspended trade talks with Israel, the Israeli ambassador to the UK has been summoned to the foreign office and fresh sanctions have been imposed on some settlers in the West Bank. Hundreds of people have been killed in Gaza over the past few days. Also: senior figures in the government discuss whether to row back on the policy restricting winter fuel payments. And the British man, William Goodge, claims to have set a new record for running across Australia.

title: John Campbell to the rescue! (The UK/EU deal explained)
summary: Will food prices come down? Will there be changes in migration? Can we buy more plants? And what of the ВЈ200 million spent on brand new port facilities in NI after Brexit? In other words, what will the new EU/UK deal mean for Northern Ireland? Our Economics and Business Editor John Campbell give Tara Mills and Declan Harvey the lowdown on what all of this means for us.  Also on todayвҖҷs episode: -	Does John have a photographic memory? -	TaraвҖҷs days writing for Ceefax  -	Did Declan break the law?  Get in touch: thestateofus@bbc.co.uk

title: Brexit all over again?
summary: With the media responding to Sir Keir Starmer's new landmark UK-EU deal, the question that James wants answered is, can we avoid falling into old traps, or are we destined to bow to the right-wing media like before? With several businesses publicly confirming that this new deal will benefit the majority of us, will the nation move forward and listen to those trusted voices, or will they be swayed by those with the most to gain?В  This episode was recorded on the 20th May. Catch James O'Brien weekdays from 10am on LBC.

title: Brexit is dead. Long live the future
summary: The Matts are on something of a high. This weekвҖҷs EU deal suddenly feels much more consequential than theyвҖҷd expected. Credit (for once) to Starmer вҖҰ even in his technocratic monotone, he presided over a clear vibe shift in the UKвҖҷs relationship with the EU. No, it doesnвҖҷt go nearly far enough. No, itвҖҷs not rejoin. But yes вҖҰ itвҖҷs a new pathway and it feels like we have, finally, a PM willing to start fixing the mess of the last 9 years. The hard yards start now, but at least we can be confident that weвҖҷve won the argument, Brexit is definitively a dud, and the future beckons. Enjoy. EXCLUSIVE OFFER: Get The New European for just ВЈ1 for the first month. Head toВ theneweuropean.co.uk/2matts  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Keir Starmer hails mood change after EU-UK reset deal
summary: Keir Starmer claims Britain is back on the world stage after securing a new deal between Britain and the EU - but opposition leaders say he has surrendered on fishing rights.  Chief political correspondent Rachael Burford has the latest. And in part two, The London StandardвҖҷs business editor Jonathan Pyrnn reveals details of the Barbican CentreвҖҷs ambitious renovation.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Britain suspends trade talks with Israel
summary: The frustration in Keir Starmer's voice was palpable. HeвҖҷs joined forces with France and Canada to pressure Israel to stop the humanitarian disaster unfolding in Gaza. How far are these leaders prepared to go? And will Trump support them?  Later, what is the political fallout from the EU trade deal? And what would the Lib DemвҖҷs do differently? We hear from Ed Davey on Brexit, Nick Clegg and his new book вҖңWhy I careвҖң. The News Agents is brought to you by HSBC UK - https://www.hsbc.co.uk/

title: Tories Sink In The Polls
summary: The Tories have fallen to fourth place in the latest Times/YouGov poll - behind the Liberal Democrats for the first time in six years. Plus, Keir Starmer might've landed his EU reset deal, but Boris Johnson has labelled him the 'orange-ball chewing gimp of Brussels'. Hugo Rifkind unpacks the politics of the day with Janice Turner and James Marriott.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: What has reaction been to the UK-EU deal?
summary: Fallout continues from yesterday's summit and the announcement of a deal between the UK and EU вҖ“ or is it fair to call it 'fallout' as, despite criticism over the deal from Nigel Farage and Kemi Badenoch, has the public got Brexit fatigue?В  James Heale and Michael Simmons join Patrick Gibbons to talk about the reaction to the deal. Fisheries has taken up most discussion but Michael points out a lesser talked about commitment to energy policy. And, with the government keen to talk about it in tandem with recent deals with India and the US вҖ“ and Gulf states soon, according to Rachel Reeves this morning вҖ“ what's the political narrative around the summit? Produced by Patrick Gibbons.

title: British public have WOKEN UP and regret voting Labour
summary: 'The public now has really awakened to the fact that many regret voting Labour and that Starmer wants more to be close to the EU than to think about British people!' Life Peer Baroness Hoey says the Prime Minister is turning his back on Red Wall voters with a Brexit reset deal.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: KemiвҖҷs Tories drop to 4th
summary: Sky NewsвҖҷ Sam Coates and PoliticoвҖҷs Anne McElvoy serve up their essential guide to the day in British politics.В В  It'll be a day when the PM goes again at selling the merits of his EU deal. One former cabinet minister tells the podcast it's all 'concrete and waffle'.В  Welfare is on the agenda today too - with a DWP committee session on child welfare.В  And, did someone mention Nick Clegg? The former Deputy PM has used the c-word about the Lib Dems. Sam and Anne discuss coalition after another favorable poll for them - but it's a poll which really isn't good news for the Tories

title: бһ–бһҸбҹҢбһҳбһ¶бһ“бһ”бҹ’бһҡбһ…бһ¶бҹҶбһҗбҹ’бһ„бҹғбһ“бҹғбһҹбһ¶бһҡбһ–бҹҗбһҸбҹҢбһҳбһ¶бһ“ бһҢбһ№ бһҒбҹҒбһҳбһ”бһјбһҢбһ¶ бһҠбҹҒбһӣбһёбһҹбһҳбҹ’бһҡбһ¶бһ”бҹӢбһҡбһ¶бһҸбҹ’бһҡбһёбһҗбҹ’бһ„бҹғбһ‘бһёбҹЎбҹ© бһҒбҹӮбһ§бһҹбһ—бһ¶ бһҶбҹ’бһ“бһ¶бҹҶбҹўбҹ бҹўбҹҘ
summary: бҹЎ) вҖӢвҖӢбһўбҹ’бһ“бһҖбһғбҹ’бһӣбһ¶бҹҶбһҳбһҫбһӣбҹ–бһ”бһҫбһҹбһҳбһҷбһ»бһ‘бҹ’бһ’бһ“бһ¶бһӮбһҳбһ¶бһҹбһҠбһҫбһҡбһ бһҪбһҹбһ–бһёвҖӢбһӮбҹ„бһӣбһҖбһ¶бһҡбһҺбҹҚвҖӢбһўбһ¶бһ–бҹ’бһҷбһ¶бһҖбҹ’бһҡбһ№бһҸбһ“бһ№бһ„бһ“бһ¶бҹҶбһ‘бһ»бһҖбҹ’бһҒбһҠбһ¶бһҖбҹӢбһҒбҹ’бһӣбһҪбһ“бҹ” бҹў) вҖӢ бһўбһ¶бһҮбҹ’бһүбһ¶бһ’бһҡвҖӢ бһ“бһ№бһ„бһ”бҹҶбһ–бһ¶бһҖбҹӢбһ”бһ“бҹ’бһҗбҹӮбһҳбһ§бһ”бһҖбһҡбһҺбҹҚбһҖбһ¶бһҡбһ–бһ¶бһҡбһҡбһ“бҹ’бһ‘бҹҮбһ“бҹ…бһҸбһ¶бһҳвҖӢбһ”бҹ’бһҡбһ¶бһҹбһ¶бһ‘вҖӢбһ”бһ“бҹ’бһ‘бһ¶бһ”бҹӢвҖӢбһ–бһёбһҳбһ¶бһ“бһ”бһ¶бһҸбһ»бһ—бһјбһҸбһҡбһ“бҹ’бһ‘бҹҮбһ”бһ¶бһүбҹӢбһ”бһҺбҹ’бһҠбһ¶бһӣбһұбҹ’бһҷбһҳбһ“бһ»бһҹбҹ’бһҹбһҹбҹ’бһӣбһ¶бһ”бҹӢбҹ” бҹЈ) вҖӢ бһ”бһҖбҹ’бһҹбһ”бҹ’бһҡбһҶбһ¶бҹҶбһ„бһҹбҹ’бһ“бһҫбһұбҹ’бһҷ бһӣбҹ„бһҖ бһ бҹҠбһ»бһ“ бһҳбҹүбһ¶бһҺбҹӮбһҸ бһҠбһ¶бһҖбҹӢбһҹбҹҶбһҺбһҫбһ‘бҹ…бһҡбһҠбҹ’бһӢвҖӢбһҹбһ—бһ¶вҖӢбһ’бҹ’бһңбһҫбһҹбһ…бҹ’бһ…бһ¶бһ”бҹҗбһ“бһӣбһҫбһ…бҹ’бһ”бһ¶бһ”бҹӢбһҹбһҳбһ»бһ‘бҹ’бһҡбҹ” бҹӨ) вҖӢ бһӣбҹ„бһҖ бһ бҹҠбһ»бһ“ бһҳбҹүбһ¶бһҺбҹӮбһҸбһўбҹҶбһ–бһ¶бһңбһ“бһ¶бһңбһұбҹ’бһҷ бһ–бһӣбһҡбһҠбҹ’бһӢбһҒбҹ’бһҳбҹӮбһҡбһ…бһјбһӣбһҡбһҪбһҳвҖӢбһҗбҹӮвҖӢбһҡбһҖбҹ’бһҹбһ¶вҖӢбһҹбһ“бҹ’бһҸбһ·бһ—бһ¶бһ–бһҠбһҫбһҳбҹ’бһ”бһёбһҖбһ»бҹҶбһұбҹ’бһҷбһҡбһ”бһ”бһ”бҹ’бһҡбһӣбҹҗбһҷ бһ–бһјбһҮбһҹбһ¶бһҹбһ“бҹҚбһңбһ·бһӣбһҳбһҖбһңбһ·бһүбҹ” бҹҘ) вҖӢ HRW бһҗбһ¶ бһҮбһ”бҹүбһ»бһ“бһҳбһ·бһ“бһҸбҹ’бһҡбһјбһңбһўбҹ„бһ”бһҠбҹғбһҲбһҳбһҫбһӣвҖӢбһҡбһҠбҹ’бһӢбһ¶бһ—бһ·бһ”бһ¶бһӣбһҖбһҳбҹ’бһ–бһ»бһҮбһ¶бһ”бһ“бҹ’бһҸвҖӢвҖӢбһҖбҹҖбһ”бһҹбһ„бҹ’бһҖбһҸбҹӢ бһ“бһ·бһ„бһҡбҹҶбһӣбҹ„бһ—вҖӢбһҹбһ·бһ‘бҹ’бһ’бһ·бһҳбһ“бһ»бһҹбҹ’бһҹбһҸбһ¶бһҳбһ‘бҹҶбһ“бһҫбһ„бһ…бһ·бһҸбҹ’бһҸбһ‘бҹҖбһҸбһ“бҹ„бҹҮбһ‘бҹҒбҹ” бҹҰ) бһҖбҹ’бһҡбҹ„бһҷбһ–бһёбһ…бһҡбһ…бһ¶вҖӢбһ”бһҡбһ¶бһҮбҹҗбһҷ бһҠбһ¶бһҺбһәбһҳбҹүбһ¶бһҖбһ•бҹ’бһҸбһӣбҹӢбһҮбҹҶбһ“бһҪбһҷбһҷбҹ„бһ’бһ¶ бҹҰбҹ бҹ бһӣбһ¶бһ“вҖӢбһҠбһ»бһӣбҹ’бһӣбһ¶бһҡбһҒбһҺбҹҲбһўбҹҠбһёбһҸбһ¶бһӣбһёбһ”бһүбҹ’бһҮбһјбһ“бһҡбһҗбһ–бһ¶бһҹбһҠбҹӮбһҖбһ…бҹҶбһ“бһҪбһ“бҹӨбҹ бҹ бһӮбҹ’бһҡбһҝбһ„бһ‘бҹ…бһұбҹ’бһҷбһўбҹҠбһ»бһҷбһҖбҹ’бһҡбҹӮбһ“бҹ”

title: Britain, Biden, and One Big Beautiful Bill
summary: First, Editor-in-Chief of Breitbart News and author of "Breaking Biden," Alex Marlow, joins the show to explain the inner workings of Biden's insular world, the Hur tapes, and One Big Beautiful Bill. Next, Mike Davis from the Article 3 Project joins to discuss the path forward on immigration for the Trump administration after the Supreme Court's shock ruling to pause the Trump adminstration's use of the 1798 Alien Enemies Act to deport Tren de Aragua gang members. Finally, broadcasting LIVE from the UK, Charlie describes how making Britain conservative again will require a free speech revival.  Watch ad-free on members.charliekirk.com!  Get new merch at charliekirkstore.com! Support the show: http://www.charliekirk.com/support See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: From fishing to food exports: What's the UK's deal with the EU?
summary: The UK and the EU have reached a new deal setting out post-Brexit relations on areas including fishing rights, trade and defence. We hear from the Irish fishing community, a food exporter in the UK and what a "youth experience scheme" could mean for businesses in France. Also, Andrew Peach finds out how one of the most important companies in the world is helping shape the future of the global economy.
